flags.description: The requirement mandates use of 'SLAM' — a specific algorithmic approach — rather than stating the need (autonomous 2D indoor map generation). SLAM is an implementation choice that should be left to design. The requirement should specify WHAT capability is needed, not HOW to achieve it.
flags.recommendation: Rewrite as: 'The system shall autonomously generate and maintain a 2D indoor map of the operating environment.' Leave SLAM as a design decision in the architecture documentation.

flags.description: Localization accuracy of ±5 cm is specified without defining the reference frame, environmental conditions (e.g., room size, feature density), or operating mode under which this accuracy must be achieved. Additionally, it is unclear whether this is a static or dynamic (moving) accuracy requirement.
flags.recommendation: Add conditions: e.g., 'The system shall localize itself with ±5 cm accuracy (95th percentile) relative to a previously built map, in a room of at least 10 m², under normal indoor lighting conditions, while navigating at operational speed.'

flags.description: The term 'real-time' is used without a numeric bound. 'Real-time' is ambiguous — two engineers may implement 10 ms or 500 ms response cycles and both claim compliance. Additionally, 'avoid' does not specify minimum clearance distance or the conditions under which avoidance must succeed.
flags.recommendation: Replace 'in real-time' with an explicit latency bound, e.g., 'The system shall detect and initiate an avoidance maneuver for static and dynamic obstacles within [X] ms of detection, maintaining a minimum clearance of [Y] cm.'

flags.description: The requirement combines two behaviors: (1) detect cliffs/stairs and (2) prevent falling. The threshold for cliff detection (e.g., drop depth ≥ X cm) is not specified, and 'prevent falling' does not define the stopping distance or reaction time. This is also an atomicity concern as it bundles detection and prevention.
flags.recommendation: Split into two atomic requirements: (1) 'The system shall detect a floor drop of ≥ [X] cm within [Y] cm of the edge.' (2) 'The system shall stop all drive motors within [Z] ms of detecting a cliff condition.' Specify the detection threshold.

flags.description: 'Multi-room mapping and segmentation' does not specify the maximum number of rooms supported, the criteria for room boundary detection, or how segmentation is presented to the user. Without these, the requirement cannot be tested.
flags.recommendation: Add a minimum room count (e.g., 'at least [N] rooms'), define segmentation criteria (e.g., doorways, user-defined boundaries), and specify how segmented rooms are identified (e.g., labeled on the map).

flags.description: The suction power requirement (>= 5000 Pa) lacks measurement conditions. Suction pressure depends on nozzle geometry, measurement method, and test setup. Without a defined test standard or condition (e.g., sealed suction, at nozzle inlet, per IEC 62885-2), the value is not reproducibly testable.
flags.recommendation: Specify measurement conditions and reference a test standard: e.g., 'The system shall generate a sealed suction pressure of ≥ 5000 Pa, measured at the nozzle inlet per [test method/standard].'

flags.description: 'Automatically adjust suction based on detected floor type' does not specify: (a) which floor types trigger which suction levels, (b) what the suction levels are for each floor type, or (c) the response time for the adjustment. The requirement is not testable as written.
flags.recommendation: Add a mapping table: e.g., 'The system shall set suction power to [X] Pa on hard floors and [Y] Pa on carpet surfaces within [Z] ms of floor type detection.' Define all supported floor types.

flags.description: The requirement bundles two distinct behaviors: (1) detecting an empty water tank, and (2) notifying the user. These are separate functions and should be traceable, tested, and implemented independently.
flags.recommendation: Split into: (1) 'The system shall detect when the water tank is empty.' (2) 'The system shall notify the user within [X] seconds of detecting an empty water tank condition.'

flags.description: 'Resume cleaning after recharge' does not specify: (a) the battery level at which resumption occurs, (b) whether it resumes from the interrupted position or restarts, (c) whether resumption is automatic or requires user confirmation, or (d) the maximum time before resumption.
flags.recommendation: Specify: 'The system shall automatically resume the interrupted cleaning task from the last recorded position when battery charge reaches ≥ [X]% after returning to dock, without user intervention.'

flags.description: SYS-FUNC-032 ('support automatic charging') is largely subsumed by SYS-FUNC-030 (auto-return to dock when battery < 20%) and SYS-FUNC-031 (resume after recharge). If 'automatic charging' means something beyond auto-return and docking, it is not differentiated. If it is the same behavior, it is redundant and creates maintenance risk.
flags.recommendation: Clarify what 'automatic charging' means beyond SYS-FUNC-030. If it refers to the act of initiating charging upon docking, make that explicit and distinct. Otherwise, consider removing this requirement.

flags.description: 'Support cleaning scheduling' is underspecified. It does not define: granularity (daily, weekly, one-time), maximum number of schedules, persistence across power cycles, or how conflicts are handled.
flags.recommendation: Add details: e.g., 'The system shall allow the user to configure up to [N] recurring cleaning schedules (specifying day of week and start time), which shall persist across power cycles.'

flags.description: 'Provide cleaning reports (area, duration, battery usage)' does not specify how reports are delivered (app, local display, stored log), the retention period, or the format/units of each data element.
flags.recommendation: Specify delivery mechanism, retention period, and units: e.g., 'The system shall generate a cleaning report for each completed session, accessible via the mobile application, containing: cleaned area (m²), session duration (minutes), and battery consumption (%), retained for at least [N] sessions.'

flags.description: 'Limit brush torque to prevent injury' does not specify the maximum allowable torque value or the conditions under which the limit applies. Without a quantitative threshold, this safety requirement cannot be tested or verified, which is a critical gap under ISO 26262.
flags.recommendation: Specify the maximum brush torque value in N·m (or equivalent) and the detection/response mechanism: e.g., 'The system shall limit brush motor torque to ≤ [X] N·m under all operating conditions. If applied torque exceeds [X] N·m, the brush motor shall stop within [Y] ms.'

flags.description: 'Prevent battery overcharge and deep discharge' lacks quantitative thresholds. The upper charge voltage limit and the lower discharge cutoff (in volts or % SoC) are not defined. Without these, the requirement is not verifiable and does not provide sufficient guidance for implementation.
flags.recommendation: Specify thresholds: e.g., 'The system shall disconnect the charging circuit when battery cell voltage exceeds [X] V per cell. The system shall stop all operations and enter safe-state when battery State of Charge (SoC) drops below [Y]%.' Reference applicable battery safety standards (e.g., IEC 62133).

flags.description: 'Monitor motor temperature and prevent overheating' does not define: (a) the temperature threshold(s) at which action is taken, (b) which motors are monitored, (c) the corrective action (reduce power, stop, alert user), or (d) the maximum response time. This renders the requirement untestable.
flags.recommendation: Specify: 'The system shall monitor the temperature of [drive motors / vacuum motor / all motors]. If any monitored motor temperature exceeds [X]°C, the system shall [reduce power / stop the motor] within [Y] ms and notify the user.'

flags.description: 'Cleaning coverage efficiency >= 95%' does not define: (a) the test environment (room geometry, obstacle density), (b) the floor type, (c) the number of passes allowed, or (d) how 'coverage' is measured (area swept vs. area cleaned to a cleanliness standard).
flags.recommendation: Add test conditions: e.g., 'Cleaning coverage efficiency shall be ≥ 95% of the navigable floor area, measured in a rectangular room of [X] m² with [N] fixed obstacles, on a hard floor surface, in a single cleaning session.'

flags.description: Maximum noise level <= 65 dB does not specify the measurement standard (e.g., IEC 60704-2-1 for floor care appliances), measurement distance, operating mode (which suction power level), or A-weighting (dB(A)).
flags.recommendation: Specify: e.g., 'Maximum noise level shall be ≤ 65 dB(A), measured at 1 m distance per [IEC 60704-2-1], at maximum suction power setting.'

flags.description: 'Operating time >= 120 minutes' does not specify the operating conditions: suction power level, floor type, room temperature, battery age/condition, or whether mopping is active. Results will vary significantly across these conditions.
flags.recommendation: Add conditions: e.g., 'Operating time shall be ≥ 120 minutes on a fully charged battery at [medium/standard] suction power setting on hard floor, at [20–25°C] ambient temperature, with new battery.'

flags.description: 'Charging time <= 4 hours' does not specify the initial SoC (e.g., from fully depleted), ambient temperature conditions, or the charging state considered 'complete' (100% SoC or a usable threshold).
flags.recommendation: Specify: e.g., 'Charging time from 0% to 100% SoC shall be ≤ 4 hours at [20–25°C] ambient temperature using the supplied charging dock.'

flags.description: 'Support secure OTA firmware updates' is underspecified. 'Secure' is not defined in this context. Missing: integrity verification method (signature algorithm), rollback protection, authentication of the update source, and behavior on failed update.
flags.recommendation: Expand to cover distinct verifiable behaviors: e.g., 'The system shall verify the digital signature of any OTA firmware package before installation, using [algorithm]. The system shall reject and discard any firmware package that fails signature verification. The system shall maintain the current firmware version if an OTA update fails.'

flags.description: The requirement mandates a 'LIDAR or vision-based navigation sensor', which prescribes specific sensor technologies rather than stating the navigation sensing capability needed. Unless LIDAR/vision is a contractual or safety-driven constraint, this overconstrains the design.
flags.recommendation: If sensor technology is not contractually mandated, rewrite as a capability requirement: e.g., 'The system shall include a sensor capable of detecting obstacles and generating spatial data sufficient to support 2D SLAM-based navigation.' If LIDAR is mandated, document the rationale.

flags.description: SYS-SW-001 mandates a specific three-layer software architecture (Application / Middleware / HAL). This is an implementation architecture decision and not a system-level need. At system requirements level, prescribing internal SW architecture is an overconstraint that restricts design freedom without adding verified value.
flags.recommendation: If architectural layering is a genuine system-level constraint (e.g., for safety isolation or third-party integration), retain with documented rationale. Otherwise, move this to the SW architecture design document and replace at system level with the capability it enables (e.g., 'The software shall support independent updating of hardware-specific and application-specific modules.').

flags.description: 'Support modular firmware updates' does not define what 'modular' means in this context (individual subsystem updates vs. monolithic), nor does it specify constraints on partial-update integrity, atomicity, or the minimum granularity of an updatable module.
flags.recommendation: Specify: e.g., 'The system shall support independent firmware updates for each of the following modules: [list]. An update to one module shall not require re-flashing unrelated modules.' Add integrity and rollback requirements.

flags.description: 'Log diagnostic events with timestamps' does not specify: (a) which events qualify as 'diagnostic events', (b) timestamp resolution and reference (UTC or relative), (c) log storage capacity or retention duration, (d) how logs are accessed/exported, or (e) behavior when log storage is full.
flags.recommendation: Specify at minimum: event categories to be logged, timestamp format and resolution (e.g., UTC, 1-second resolution), minimum log retention (e.g., last 1000 events or 30 days), and log access mechanism (e.g., via mobile app or local interface).

flags.description: SYS-FUNC-004 (cliff detection function) and SYS-HW-002 (cliff detection sensors) address the same capability but are not explicitly linked by a traceability tag. If upstream requirements management does not enforce this trace, the functional requirement may be implemented without its hardware dependency being tracked.
flags.recommendation: Ensure traceability links are established between SYS-FUNC-004 and SYS-HW-002 in the requirements management tool.

flags.description: 'Electronically controlled water dispensing' — is there a quantitative flow rate range or dispensing precision requirement? Without a numeric bound, this requirement cannot be fully verified as distinct from SYS-FUNC-021 which specifies Low/Medium/High levels.
flags.recommendation: Clarify whether SYS-FUNC-020 is intended to be a higher-level capability statement subsumed by SYS-FUNC-021, or if it carries additional requirements (e.g., on/off response time, flow precision). If the former, consider removing or marking as an informational parent.

flags.description: 'Adjustable water flow levels (Low/Medium/High)' does not define the actual flow rates (ml/min) for each level, making the requirement non-quantitatively testable. The distinction between levels cannot be verified without numeric bounds.
flags.recommendation: Add numeric bounds for each level: e.g., 'Low: [X–Y] ml/min, Medium: [Y–Z] ml/min, High: [Z–W] ml/min.' Specify tolerance.

flags.description: 'Lift the mop automatically when carpet is detected' does not specify: (a) the minimum lift height to prevent carpet wetting, (b) the response time from detection to full lift, or (c) whether water dispensing is also halted. Without these, water damage to carpet is not reliably prevented.
flags.recommendation: Specify: 'The system shall lift the mop to a height of ≥ [X] mm and cease water dispensing within [Y] ms of detecting a carpet surface.' Cross-reference SYS-FUNC-012 for carpet detection.

flags.description: 'Return to charging dock when battery < 20%' does not specify: (a) behavior if the dock is unreachable (blocked path), (b) whether the current cleaning task is paused or abandoned, or (c) what happens if the battery is depleted before reaching the dock.
flags.recommendation: Add failure mode handling: e.g., 'If the dock is unreachable within [X] minutes, the system shall stop in place and alert the user. The system shall save the current cleaning task state for resumption.'

flags.description: 'Stop all drive motors within 100 ms if lifted' does not specify: (a) the sensor/mechanism used to detect lifting, (b) whether brush and vacuum motors are also stopped, (c) the minimum lift displacement that triggers the response, or (d) behavior after being set back down.
flags.recommendation: Clarify scope: 'The system shall detect a lift condition (defined as [X] mm of wheel displacement) and stop all drive motors within 100 ms. Specify whether brush/vacuum motors are also stopped and whether restarting requires user action.'

flags.description: 'Prevent operation if the dustbin is removed' does not define: (a) which operations are prevented (all motors? only vacuum?), (b) the detection mechanism or timing, (c) behavior when dustbin is re-inserted (automatic resume or user-initiated), or (d) whether an alert is issued.
flags.recommendation: Specify: 'The system shall detect the absence of the dustbin within [X] ms and shall stop the vacuum motor and brush motor within [Y] ms. The system shall not resume vacuuming until the dustbin is re-inserted and confirmed seated.'
